title: Grounding review for issues #1347-#1350 closeout and Planning repairs
scope: Workflow obligations, Planning closeout stale active state, lane activation projection, and workspace front-door lane lifecycle forwarding
goal: Ground the implementation for #1347-#1350 so closure is based on observed gaps and targeted proof.
non_goals: Do not redesign Planning lifecycle semantics beyond the four issue acceptances.
Do not add a new workflow-obligation engine or a new Planning state store.

review_mode.review question: Which existing AW surfaces fail to make workflow obligations, completed active state, active slice execplans, and lane lifecycle commands visible and repairable?

review_method.commands used: AW start/implement, GitHub issue inspection, focused source/test reads, and focused regression tests.
review_method.evidence sources: workspace runtime primitive source, Planning bootstrap installer source, workspace CLI/report tests, Planning lane/summary tests, and issue acceptance criteria for #1347-#1350.

findings.title: Closeout reports did not give workflow obligations a user-visible state contract
findings.summary: Workflow obligations could exist in config/startup flow without a compact closeout contract that final-response rendering must include before claiming completion.
findings.evidence: closeout_report final-response rendering had no workflow-obligation required fact; configured obligations such as dogfooding_lane_closeout could be omitted from the chat-facing closeout scaffold.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can satisfy internal mechanics while still failing the user-facing obligation report that motivated the lane.
findings.suggested action: Add a closeout workflow_obligation_contract with explicit states and make final_response_rendering include required obligation status.

findings.title: Completed execplans could remain reachable through active Planning state
findings.summary: Planning summary warned about historical completed execplan references, but it did not specifically identify active rows pointing at completed execplans or offer a direct cleanup route.
findings.evidence: Active state can retain a todo item whose execplan file has completed status; the close-item repair path did not normalize the legacy status spelling complete as completed.
findings.risk if unchanged: Future work may resume or route through stale active state even though later closeout evidence says the run completed.
findings.suggested action: Detect stale_completed_active_state, suggest close-item/closeout repair, and normalize complete as completed for cleanup.

findings.title: Lane activation did not project active slice execplan ownership
findings.summary: Activating a lane with a current slice could project lane metadata without carrying the active slice execplan reference into the roadmap row used by summary/doctor.
findings.evidence: Lane state contains slice_sequence execplan_ref; roadmap lane projection omitted current_slice execplan and registration did not count roadmap execplan refs.
findings.risk if unchanged: A valid active lane can look incomplete or unregistered, and agents lose the concrete slice execplan pointer needed for continuation.
findings.suggested action: Project current_slice and execplan from the active slice, then treat roadmap execplan refs as registered live references.

findings.title: Workspace Planning front door dropped lane lifecycle positionals
findings.summary: The workspace CLI parser accepted lane lifecycle arguments, but the primitive adapter did not forward lane ids and lane-specific options to the Planning module.
findings.evidence: lane-activate, lane-close, and lane-archive require a lane positional; the adapter only forwarded common target/format style options for many planning subcommands.
findings.risk if unchanged: Users can invoke a seemingly valid workspace command that fails or mutates the wrong shape because required lifecycle arguments are lost.
findings.suggested action: Forward lane lifecycle positionals and lane-specific options through _planning_module_argv.

recommendation.promote: #1347-#1350 are implementation-ready as one bounded lane because all findings map to existing surfaces and focused regression tests.
recommendation.defer: No broader workflow-obligation redesign or Planning lifecycle model redesign is needed for this lane.

retention.proof surface: routed issues, planning state, docs, checks, Memory, or a compact retained review stub

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.
